title: Electronic device for controlling lighting
abstract: A smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) adapted to be plugged into a power socket is disclosed. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) includes a housing (202), a motion detecting sensor (204) disposed in the housing (202), a Light Dependent Resistor (LDR) (206) disposed in the housing (202), and a controller (208) in communication with the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206). The motion detecting sensor (204) is adapted to detect a movement and intensity of ambient light within a first predefined range of distance and a second predefined range of distance, respectively. The controller (208) is adapted to receive at least one value from at least one of the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206). The value is indicative of the detection of at least one of movement and the intensity of ambient light. The controller (208) is adapted to compare the value with a predefined threshold value, and operate a light unit (106) disposed in the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), based on the comparison.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) adapted to be plugged into a power socket, the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) comprising: a housing (202); a motion detecting sensor (204) disposed in the housing (202) and adapted to detect a movement within a first predefined range of distance from the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); a Light Dependent Resistor (LDR) (206) disposed in the housing (202) and adapted to detect intensity of ambient light within a second predefined range of distance from the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); and a controller (208) in communication with the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206), and adapted to: receive at least one value from at least one of the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206), wherein the at least one value is indicative of the detection of at least one of movement and the intensity of ambient light; compare the at least one value with a predefined threshold value for the movement and the intensity of ambient light; and operate a light unit (106) disposed in the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), based on the comparison, wherein the light unit (106) is adapted to illuminate the surroundings of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800). 2. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the controller (208) is adapted to activate the light unit (106) to illuminate the surroundings of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), when the at least one value is greater than the predefined threshold value. 3. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the controller (208) is adapted to deactivate the light unit (106), when the at least one value is less than the predefined threshold value. 4. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising: a Fresnel lens (214) disposed in the housing (202) to partially surround the motion detecting sensor (204), wherein the Fresnel lens (214) is positioned such that the ambient light is incident on the motion detecting sensor (204) for detection; and 

 a cover (216) disposed on the housing (202) at a periphery of the Fresnel lens (214) and adapted to partially cover (216) the motion detecting sensor (204), wherein the cover (216) is positioned to restrict incidence of the light emitted by the light unit (106) on the Fresnel lens (214). 5. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising a turn-table mechanism (222) adapted to engage with the power socket, such that the housing (202) of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) is adapted to rotate about the turn-table mechanism (222). 6. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising a removable holder attachment (210, 804, 1202, 1802) adapted to be disposed on the housing (202) to accommodate the light unit (106) on the housing (202). 7. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 6, wherein the removable holder attachment (210, 804, 1202, 1802) is adapted to directly connect to the power socket for operating the light unit (106). 8. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising an air vent (224) formed on the housing (202) to dissipate excess heat generated within the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800). 9. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising a timer disposed in the housing (202) and in communication with the light unit (106), wherein the timer is adapted to be operated for selecting a time duration of illumination of the light unit (106), upon detection by at least one of the motion detecting sensor (204) and LDR (206). 10. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising an in-built lamp (218) adapted to operate independently of the light unit (106). 11. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 9, further comprising: a first button (602) adapted to control a sensitivity of movement to be detected by the motion detecting sensor (204); a second button (604) adapted to set the timer; a third button (606) adapted to control the intensity of illumination of the light unit (106), wherein the first button (602), the second button (604), and the third button (606) are disposed on the housing (202); and 

 a knob (608) adapted to switch between a plurality of operational modes of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), wherein an operational mode is indicative of operational status of at least one of an in-built lamp (218), the light unit (106), the motion detecting sensor (204), and the LDR (206). 12. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, wherein operations are controlled by at least one of a plurality of buttons (602, 604, 606) and knobs (608) disposed on the housing (202), an application (104) in wireless connection with the controller (208), and a remote device. 13. The smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the motion detecting sensor (204) includes at least a Passive Infrared (PIR) sensor. 14. A smart electronic apparatus (100) comprising: a smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) adapted to be plugged into a power socket, the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) comprising: a housing (202); a motion detecting sensor (204) disposed in the housing (202) and adapted to detect a movement within a first predefined range of distance from the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); a Light Dependent Resistor (LDR) (206) disposed in the housing (202) and adapted to detect intensity of ambient light within a second predefined range of distance from the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); and a controller (208) in communication with the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206), and adapted to: receive at least one value from at least one of the motion detecting sensor (204) and the LDR (206), wherein the at least one value is indicative of the detection of at least one of movement and the intensity of ambient light; compare the at least one value with a predefined threshold value for the movement and the intensity of ambient light; and operate a light unit (106) disposed in the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), based on the comparison, wherein the light unit (106) is adapted to illuminate the surroundings of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); and a system (104) in communication with the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), the system (104) comprising: 

 a receiving module (710) to receive details pertaining to operation of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800); and a transmitting module adapted to transmit instructions to wirelessly control at least one operational parameter of the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800), wherein the at least one operational parameter includes one of a sensitivity of movement to be detected by the motion detecting sensor (204), timer settings, operational states of the light unit (106), and intensity of illumination of the light unit (106). 15. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, wherein the system (104) is adapted to be installed in at least one of a smart phone, a tablet, and a computing device. 16. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, wherein the system (104) is in communication with the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) through BluetoothÂ® connection. 17. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, wherein the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) comprises an alarm clock adapted to be operated by the system (104). 18. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, wherein the smart electronic device (102, 800, 1200, 1800) comprising at least one image formed on a front face. 19. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, further comprising a removable holder attachment (210, 804, 1202, 1802) adapted to be disposed on the housing (202) to accommodate the light unit (106) on the housing (202). 20. The smart electronic apparatus (100) as claimed in claim 14, further comprising a second light unit (2200) adapted to connect to an integrated socket disposed on the housing (202), without the holder attachment (210, 804, 1202, 1802).
